title: Kick the Low-Man Tag — Other Side, Same Corner
game_context: 5-on-5 half-court. You are the point guard initiating a high ball-screen at the top of the key. Your screener (5) sets the screen on your LEFT and rolls hard. The screen defender (x5) drops in textbook drop coverage. The weak-side low man (x2, guarding the weak corner on the RIGHT) stunts up to tag the roller — and this rep he commits all the way: both feet inside the lane, chest square to the roller, hips no longer shading his man in the corner. Same coverage cluster as DROP-03, mirrored to the left-side screen.
possession_setup: Top-of-the-key high pick-and-roll, screen on the LEFT. x5 drops textbook depth — chest at the screen level, feet to his own rim. x1 chases on the user's hip. As the screener rolls into the lane, x2 (weak corner defender on the RIGHT) abandons his stance and steps fully into the tag line. The strong-side help (x4, left corner) stays attached to his corner; the wing defender (x3) is shell-honest.
decision_moment: You come off the LEFT-side screen, see the drop, and look for the pocket — but the second read tells you more. The weak-side low man has committed to the tag: both feet in the lane, chest square to your roller. His RIGHT corner is empty. The high-EV read is the cross-court kick to the open RIGHT-side weak corner shooter (2), not the pocket pass to the tagged roller and not the pull-up.
visible_cue: x5 sits back in textbook drop (chest at screen level, feet to rim). x2 has stunted up so far his chest is square to the roller and BOTH feet are inside the lane — full commit, same as on the right-side base. The RIGHT-side corner behind him is wide open. The pocket pass to the roller would walk straight into x2's chest; the pull-up is fine but a corner three is the bigger advantage. Same cue cluster as DROP-03; the side flips.
prompt: Screen comes from the left. The big drops. The right-side low man steps all the way up onto the roller. What's the read?

choices.label: Skip the cross-court kick to the weak corner.
choices.feedback_text: Best read. Same kick, other side — the low man committed and his RIGHT corner is empty. Cross-court skip to 2 for the open three before x2 can recover diagonally.

choices.label: Pull up in the pocket.
choices.feedback_text: Okay. The drop still gives you the pocket pull-up on either side, but a fully-committed tag opens a corner three — bigger advantage than a mid-range pull-up.
choices.partial_feedback_text: Pull-up is open against a settled drop. The committed tag opens a wider window on the other side: the kick is the higher EV.

choices.label: Pocket pass to the roller.
choices.feedback_text: Not yet. The tag is fully committed — the pocket pass walks the roller straight into x2's chest, same as on the right side. Read the tag before you feed.

choices.label: Swing the ball back to the strong wing.
choices.feedback_text: Not yet. The swing wastes the kick window — the tag recovers before the ball gets to the corner. Hit the open shooter directly, either side.

best_read: Skip the cross-court pass to the RIGHT-side weak corner. x2 committed both feet to the tag and his chest is square to the roller — by definition his man in the corner is alone. The drop already kept x5 deep, so no one is going to close out from the paint in time. The shooter receives the ball with a full beat to gather and rise before x2 can recover diagonally across the floor. Same exploit as DROP-03 — the side flips, the cue cluster doesn't.
acceptable_reads: Pull up in the pocket — drop still gives you the pull-up, but a fully-committed tag opens the corner wider than the pocket. Lower EV than the skip kick, regardless of the screen side.
bad_reads: Pocket pass to the roller — the tag is sitting on the roller's chest, the pass gets picked or fouls the catch.
Swing back to the strong wing — the ball travel time lets x2 recover; the kick window closes.
common_miss_reason: Treating each side of the floor as a new puzzle at D3. Players who learned DROP-03 on the right side anchor on the right-side corner shooter and never re-orient their kick when the screen flips left. The chest-square + both-feet-in-the-lane tag cue is identical; only the corner the kick goes to changes.
why_best_read_works: A low-man tag is a coverage trade on either side of the floor: the helper surrenders the corner to keep the roller from finishing at the rim. When the tag is fully committed (chest square, both feet in the lane), the helper cannot recover diagonally faster than the ball can skip across the floor. The drop big stays deep and cannot rotate to the corner — that's not his job. The on-ball defender is trailing. For one beat, the corner shooter has no one on him AND no help in flight. The kick beats the help by a full beat; the catch-and-shoot lifts before x2 closes out. Mirroring the screen flips which corner is open but not the two-defender cue cluster that names the read.
decoder_teaching_point: Read two defenders on either side of the floor. The drop gives you the pocket; the tag tells you who's open BEHIND the drop. Committed tag (chest square + both feet in the lane) = kick the skip to his corner — left-side screen or right-side, the cluster is identical.

feedback.correct: Good read. Same committed tag, other side — you read his corner and the kick beat the rotation.
feedback.partial: Pull-up was open, but the corner kick was open wider, on either side. Read the tag, not just the drop.
feedback.wrong: Look at the tagger's feet AND chest. Both feet in the lane + chest square = he's gone. The side of the screen flips which corner is the answer, not whether the kick is on.

self_review_checklist: Did I read both x5 and x2 on the left-side screen too?
Did I see both of x2's feet inside the lane before I committed?
Did I make the skip pass to the RIGHT corner before x2 could recover diagonally?
Did I trust that the two-defender cluster mirrors cleanly when the screen flips sides?

coach_validation.notes: Pack 2 (Phase δ-B.M3) — DROP-03 left-side-screen mirror variant. Same low-man-tag + kick-the-corner cluster as DROP-03, mirrored across the y-axis (screen flips left, tagger and open corner flip right). Validates that D3 two-defender coverage recognition transfers across the floor; if kick choice rate drops vs DROP-03 the player anchored on the right-side geometry, not the cluster. Inherits the DROP-03 SME approval — no new cognitive surface. Single-freeze (no beatSpec.secondBeat) per DROP_DECODER_DESIGN.md §3.3.

source_research_basis: Mirror variants are the canonical post-mastery exposure pattern (see DROP-01-MIRROR, DROP-02-MIRROR, HUNT-02-MIRROR, BDW-T1-02). Same source taxonomy as DROP-03: low-man tag rotation as the canonical D3 read on top of a drop call. Kick-the-tag remains the textbook punishment for an over-committing low man, on either side of the floor.
explanation_md: **Read the Coverage — Low-Man Tag, Other Side.** Same coverage cognition as DROP-03, mirrored to a LEFT-side screen. D1 taught you the pocket pull-up. D2 taught you the snake against a deep drop. D3 adds a second defender — the weak-side low man tagging the roller. The cue cluster is still: x5's chest tells you the call (drop), x2's commitment tells you the EXPLOIT. If x2 stays in his stance and just stunts (foot in the lane but chest still angled), the pocket pass to the roller is the answer. If x2 commits fully — chest square, both feet in the lane — his corner is empty and the kick is open. **Read two defenders. Kick the committed tag. The side of the screen flips which corner is the answer, not whether the kick is the read.**

wrongDemos.caption: You pulled up in the pocket. Open shot, but the corner kick was open wider.
You fed the roller. x2 was sitting on his chest — the pass got tagged.
You swung the ball back. x2 recovered before the kick reached the corner.
